types.name: Query
types.description:
Root GraphQL Query

Combines all query types for the admin store API
All queries are automatically workspace-scoped via JWT middleware

fields.name: navigations
fields.description: List all menus for a workspace (auto-provisions Main Menu if missing)

fields.name: availableSystemRoutes
fields.description: List of hardcoded system routes (Home, Search, etc.)

fields.name: page
fields.description: Get single page by ID or Handle

fields.name: paymentMethods
fields.description: List configured payment methods for current workspace

fields.name: availableProviders
fields.description: List payment providers available to add

fields.name: storeProfile
fields.description: Get store profile settings for current workspace

fields.name: workspaceMembers
fields.description: List all workspace members with status and roles (requires staff:view permission)

fields.name: workspaceMember
fields.description: Get single workspace member details (requires staff:view permission)

fields.name: workspaceRoles
fields.description: List all workspace roles for assignment (requires staff:view permission)

fields.name: availablePermissions
fields.description: List all available permissions in the system

fields.name: pendingInvites
fields.description: List pending workspace invitations (requires staff:view permission)

fields.name: myPermissions
fields.description: Get current user's permissions in workspace

fields.name: memberPermissionSummary
fields.description: Get permission summary grouped by resource

fields.name: customers
fields.description: List all customers with pagination and filtering

fields.name: recentMedia
fields.description: Get recent media uploads for current workspace (for media picker)

args.name: mediaType
args.description: Filter by media type: image, video, 3d_model

args.name: sortBy
args.description: Sort by: date, name, size (default: date)

fields.name: bulkOperations
fields.description: Get paginated list of bulk operations

fields.name: salesChannels
fields.description: List all sales channels with pagination and filtering

fields.name: salesChannel
fields.description: Get single sales channel by ID

fields.name: channelProducts
fields.description: List channel products with pagination and filtering

fields.name: channelProduct
fields.description: Get single channel product by ID

fields.name: channelOrders
fields.description: List channel orders with pagination and filtering

fields.name: channelOrder
fields.description: Get single channel order by ID

fields.name: packages
fields.description: List all packages with pagination and filtering (for dropdown and settings)

fields.name: discounts
fields.description: List all discounts with pagination and filtering

fields.name: categories
fields.description: List all categories with pagination and filtering

fields.name: categoryProducts
fields.description: Get products in a specific category

fields.name: orders
fields.description: List all orders with pagination and filtering

fields.name: ordersBySource
fields.description: Get orders by source (whatsapp, payment, manual)

fields.name: inventory
fields.description: List all inventory with pagination and filtering

fields.name: locations
fields.description: Get all locations for authenticated workspace

fields.name: inventoryByVariant
fields.description: Get inventory for specific variant across all regions

fields.name: lowStockItems
fields.description: Get low stock items across all regions

fields.name: products
fields.description: List all products with pagination and filtering

types.name: BlogType
types.description: Blog container type (e.g. 'News')

types.name: Node
types.description: An object with an ID

types.name: ID
types.description: The `ID` scalar type represents a unique identifier, often used to refetch an object or as key for a cache. The ID type appears in a JSON response as a String; however, it is not intended to be human-readable. When expected as an input type, any string (such as `"4"`) or integer (such as `4`) input value will be accepted as an ID.

types.name: DateTime
types.description:
The `DateTime` scalar type represents a DateTime
value as specified by
[iso8601](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601).

types.name: String
types.description: The `String` scalar type represents textual data, represented as UTF-8 character sequences. The String type is most often used by GraphQL to represent free-form human-readable text.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreBlogCommentPolicyChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: Int
types.description: The `Int` scalar type represents non-fractional signed whole numeric values. Int can represent values between -(2^31) and 2^31 - 1.

fields.name: edges
fields.description: Contains the nodes in this connection.

types.name: PageInfo
types.description: The Relay compliant `PageInfo` type, containing data necessary to paginate this connection.

fields.name: hasNextPage
fields.description: When paginating forwards, are there more items?

fields.name: hasPreviousPage
fields.description: When paginating backwards, are there more items?

fields.name: startCursor
fields.description: When paginating backwards, the cursor to continue.

fields.name: endCursor
fields.description: When paginating forwards, the cursor to continue.

types.name: Boolean
types.description: The `Boolean` scalar type represents `true` or `false`.

types.name: BlogEdge
types.description: A Relay edge containing a `Blog` and its cursor.

fields.name: node
fields.description: The item at the end of the edge

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: A cursor for use in pagination

fields.description: Contains the nodes in this connection.

fields.description: The item at the end of the edge

fields.name: cursor
fields.description: A cursor for use in pagination

types.name: ArticleType
types.description: Article type for individual posts

types.name: JSONString
types.description:
Allows use of a JSON String for input / output from the GraphQL schema.

Use of this type is *not recommended* as you lose the benefits of having a defined, static
schema (one of the key benefits of GraphQL).

fields.description: Contains the nodes in this connection.

types.description: A Menu container (e.g. Main Menu).

types.name: NavigationItemType
types.description:
A single link in a menu.
Has recursive 'children' field.

fields.name: type
fields.description: Type of resource this links to

fields.name: value
fields.description: URL or Resource ID/Handle (used if FK is not set)

fields.name: url
fields.description: Computed final URL for this item

types.name: WorkspaceStoreNavigationItemTypeChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: PageType
types.description:
GraphQL type for Page model.
Represents static content pages.

types.name: CategoryType
types.description:
GraphQL type for Category model

Features:
- All category fields with proper typing
- Hierarchical parent/child relationships
- Breadcrumb navigation
- Category analytics properties
- SEO optimization fields

fields.name: isVisible
fields.description: Whether collection is visible to customers

fields.name: isFeatured
fields.description: Whether collection is featured on homepage

fields.name: sortOrder
fields.description: Manual sort order for admin drag-drop

fields.name: productCount
fields.description: Count of products in this collection

fields.name: featuredImageUrl
fields.description: Featured image URL from featured_media FK

types.name: MediaUploadType
types.description:
Media Upload GraphQL Type - URL-focused design

Returns:
- Direct URLs (not nested objects)
- Minimal metadata
- Optimized for frontend consumption

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Additional metadata (format, duration for videos, etc.)

types.name: UUID
types.description:
Leverages the internal Python implementation of UUID (uuid.UUID) to provide native UUID objects
in fields, resolvers and input.

types.name: MedialibMediaUploadMediaTypeChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: BigInt
types.description:
The `BigInt` scalar type represents non-fractional whole numeric values.
`BigInt` is not constrained to 32-bit like the `Int` type and thus is a less
compatible type.

types.name: MedialibMediaUploadStatusChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: ProductType
types.description:
GraphQL type for Product model

Features:
- All product fields with proper typing
- Variants relationship with DataLoader
- Category relationships
- Stock and analytics properties

fields.name: chargeTax
fields.description: Whether to charge tax on this product

fields.name: paymentCharges
fields.description: Whether to apply Cameroon mobile money/payment method charges

fields.name: allowBackorders
fields.description: Allow orders when out of stock

fields.name: package
fields.description: Shipping package for this product (optional - falls back to default if not set)

fields.name: featuredImageUrl
fields.description: Featured image URL from featured_media FK

types.name: Decimal
types.description: The `Decimal` scalar type represents a python Decimal.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreProductProductTypeChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreProductStatusChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreProductInventoryHealthChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: PackageType
types.description:
GraphQL type for Package model

Features:
- Simple shipping package configuration
- Region-based fees stored in JSON (multiple regions per package)
- Cameroon-specific flexibility

fields.name: regionFees
fields.description: Shipping fees by region in XAF format: {'yaounde': 1500, 'douala': 1200, 'buea': 1000}

fields.name: useAsDefault
fields.description: Use this package as default fallback for products without shipping

types.name: WorkspaceStorePackagePackageTypeChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: WorkspaceStorePackageSizeChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: ProductVariantType
types.description: GraphQL type for ProductVariant model

fields.name: featuredImageUrl
fields.description: Featured image URL from featured_media FK

types.name: Float
types.description: The `Float` scalar type represents signed double-precision fractional values as specified by [IEEE 754](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/IEEE_floating_point).

types.name: InventoryType
types.description:
GraphQL type for Inventory model

Features:
- All inventory fields with proper typing
- Variant and location relationships
- Stock status properties
- Atomic operations support
- Shopify-style inventory tracking

fields.name: variant
fields.description: Product variant for this inventory entry

fields.name: quantity
fields.description: Available stock quantity at this location (deprecated, use onhand/available)

fields.name: isAvailable
fields.description: Whether inventory is available for sale

fields.name: isLowStock
fields.description: Whether inventory is below location's low stock threshold

fields.name: productImage
fields.description: Product primary image with all variations (thumbnail, WebP, etc.)

types.name: LocationType
types.description:
GraphQL type for Location model

Features:
- All location fields with proper typing
- Inventory relationship with DataLoader
- Regional analytics properties
- Cameroon region-specific data

fields.name: totalStock
fields.description: Total stock quantity at this location

fields.name: totalValue
fields.description: Total inventory value at this location

fields.name: lowStockItems
fields.description: Number of low stock items at this location

fields.name: outOfStockItems
fields.description: Number of out-of-stock items at this location

fields.name: fullAddress
fields.description: Formatted full address including city and region

fields.name: canDeactivate
fields.description: Whether this location can be deactivated (no inventory)

types.name: WorkspaceStoreLocationRegionChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreInventoryConditionChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: SystemRouteType
types.description:
Represents a hardcoded system path (e.g. 'Search', 'Account').
Used to populate UI dropdowns.

types.name: MerchantPaymentMethodType
types.description:
GraphQL type for MerchantPaymentMethod model.

Exposes payment method configuration for workspace settings.

fields.name: checkoutUrl
fields.description: Merchant's payment checkout URL (for external redirect providers)

fields.name: enabled
fields.description: Merchant has enabled this payment method

fields.name: credentialVerifiedAt
fields.description: When merchant API credentials were last validated against the provider

types.name: ProviderCapabilitiesType
types.description:
GraphQL type for payment provider capabilities.
Nested object within MerchantPaymentMethodType.

types.name: AvailableProviderType
types.description:
GraphQL type for available payment providers.
Used when adding new payment methods.

types.name: StoreProfileType
types.description:
GraphQL type for StoreProfile model

Exposes store settings for the General Settings page.

fields.name: storeEmail
fields.description: Primary contact email for the store

fields.name: whatsappNumber
fields.description: WhatsApp number for order notifications (International format: +XXXXXXXXXXXX)

fields.name: defaultLocale
fields.description: Default language for the storefront (e.g., en, fr)

types.name: WorkspaceStoreStoreProfileCurrencyChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreStoreProfileTimezoneChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreStoreProfileWeightUnitChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: WorkspaceStoreStoreProfileDimensionUnitChoices
types.description: An enumeration.

types.name: StoreAnalytics
types.description:
Complete store analytics dashboard - tier-gated

BASIC: cards, chart, payment_breakdown
PRO: + funnel, customers
ADVANCED: + (future features)

Workspace is auto-scoped via GraphQL context (no workspace field needed).

types.name: DashboardCard
types.description:
Metric card for dashboard display - BASIC tier
Represents a single KPI with trend information

types.name: ChartData
types.description: Time-series chart data with configuration - BASIC tier

types.name: ChartDataPoint
types.description: Single data point in time-series chart - BASIC tier

types.name: ChartSeriesConfig
types.description: Chart series configurations for orders and revenue

types.name: ChartConfig
types.description: Chart series configuration

types.name: PaymentBreakdown
types.description: Payment method distribution - BASIC tier

fields.name: cashOnDelivery
fields.description: Orders paid via Cash on Delivery

types.name: ConversionFunnel
types.description:
Conversion funnel data - PRO tier
Shows customer journey: Page View -> Cart -> Checkout -> Order

types.name: FunnelStage
types.description: Single stage in conversion funnel - PRO tier

types.name: FunnelMetrics
types.description: Funnel summary metrics - PRO tier

types.name: CustomerMetrics
types.description: Customer analytics - PRO tier

types.name: DiscountType
types.description:
GraphQL type for Discount model

Features:
- All discount fields with proper typing
- Computed status properties
- Usage tracking
- Support for amount_off_product and buy_x_get_y discount types

fields.name: discountValueType
fields.description: Discount value type (percentage or fixed_amount) - for amount_off_product

fields.name: customerBuysValue
fields.description: Customer buys minimum purchase amount - for buy_x_get_y

fields.name: customerBuysProductIds
fields.description: Specific product IDs for customer buys - for buy_x_get_y

fields.name: customerGetsProductIds
fields.description: Specific product IDs for customer gets - for buy_x_get_y

fields.name: bxgyDiscountType
fields.description: Discount type for buy_x_get_y (percentage, amount_off_each, free)

fields.name: maxUsesPerOrder
fields.description: Maximum number of times discount can be used per order - for buy_x_get_y

fields.name: usageLimit
fields.description: Maximum number of times this discount can be used

fields.name: usageCount
fields.description: Number of times this discount has been used

fields.name: appliesToAllCustomers
fields.description: Whether discount applies to all customers

fields.name: appliesToAllProducts
fields.description: Whether discount applies to all products

fields.name: productIds
fields.description: Specific product IDs this discount applies to

fields.name: categoryIds
fields.description: Specific category IDs this discount applies to

fields.name: appliesToRegions
fields.description: Cameroon regions this discount applies to

fields.name: appliesToCustomerTypes
fields.description: Customer types this discount applies to

fields.name: limitTotalUses
fields.description: Whether to limit total number of uses

fields.name: limitOnePerCustomer
fields.description: Whether to limit to one use per customer

fields.name: totalDiscountAmount
fields.description: Total amount discounted across all orders

fields.name: totalOrders
fields.description: Total orders that used this discount

types.name: Mutation
types.description:
Root GraphQL Mutation

Combines all mutation types for the admin store API
All mutations use @transaction.atomic for data integrity

fields.name: menuItemCreate
fields.description: Add a link to a menu

fields.name: addPaymentMethod
fields.description:
Add a payment method to workspace.

For Fapshi: Requires checkout_url from merchant's Fapshi dashboard.
Uses atomic transaction to ensure data integrity.

fields.name: togglePaymentMethod
fields.description:
Enable or disable a payment method.

Uses atomic transaction with row-level locking.

fields.name: updatePaymentMethod
fields.description:
Update payment method configuration (e.g., checkout URL).

Uses atomic transaction with row-level locking.

fields.name: removePaymentMethod
fields.description:
Remove a payment method from workspace.

Uses atomic transaction with row-level locking.

fields.name: verifyPaymentMethod
fields.description: Manually trigger verification of payment method credentials.

fields.name: getFapshiBalance
fields.description:
Fetch Fapshi service balance for the merchant.
Requires billing:manage permission.

fields.name: updateStoreProfile
fields.description:
Update store profile settings.

Validates Cameroon phone numbers and returns proper error messages.
Uses atomic transaction for data integrity.

fields.name: uploadMedia
fields.description:
Upload media file with automatic processing (NEW FK-based system)

Flow:
1. Upload media → Get upload_id
2. Attach to entity → entity.featured_media_id = upload_id

Process:
1. User selects file in UI
2. Immediately upload to backend (entity-agnostic)
3. Return upload_id and preview URL
4. User can attach to product/category later via FK

Benefits:
- Images persist even if user cancels entity creation
- Immediately available in "Recent uploads"
- Real upload progress feedback
- Can reuse across multiple entities (just set FK)

fields.name: uploadMediaFromUrl
fields.description:
Upload media from URL (download and store)

1. Download file from URL
2. Validate and process
3. Store in MediaUpload table

User can manually manage their storage

fields.name: bulkImportProducts
fields.description:
Unified mutation for bulk product imports from ANY file type

CONSOLIDATES: CSV uploads, document uploads, image uploads
USES: ProductImportService for ALL business logic
PARSERS: Only extract data, no validation/creation

Flow:
1. Detect file type (CSV vs Document)
2. Route to appropriate parser for extraction
3. ProductImportService validates & imports
4. Return job_id for progress tracking

Performance: Async processing, returns immediately with job_id
Scalability: Handles 10,000+ products via chunked processing
Reliability: Atomic transactions, continues on errors
Security: File size limits, workspace scoping, input validation

Security: Cannot suspend self or workspace owner

fields.description:
Reactivate suspended staff member

fields.name: removeStaff

Permanently remove staff member from workspace (cannot be reactivated)

Security: Cannot remove self or workspace owner

types.name: CreateProduct
types.description:
Create product using ProductService

Core required: name, price
Optional: All other fields including variants, shipping, SEO
Performance: Atomic creation with proper locking
Security: Workspace scoping and permission validation
Reliability: Comprehensive validation and rollback

types.name: ProductCreateInput
types.description:
Input for product creation (Shopify-style)

Core required: name, price
Optional: All other fields including variants, shipping, SEO, images
Security: Workspace scoping via JWT middleware

Images: Accepts array of Upload scalars for direct file uploads

inputFields.name: variants
inputFields.description: Explicit variants with featured images and inventory

inputFields.name: mediaIds
inputFields.description: Array of media IDs for gallery (images/videos/3D models)

types.name: InventoryInput
types.description: Input for inventory-related fields (Shopify-style)

types.name: ShippingInput
types.description: Input for shipping-related fields

inputFields.name: packageId
inputFields.description: Shipping package ID (optional - falls back to default)

types.name: SEOInput
types.description: Input for SEO-related fields (Shopify-style)

inputFields.name: slug
inputFields.description: URL-friendly slug (auto-generated from name if not provided)

inputFields.name: metaTitle
inputFields.description: SEO meta title (max 60 chars, defaults to name if empty)

inputFields.name: metaDescription
inputFields.description: SEO meta description (max 160 chars, defaults to description if empty)

types.name: OrganizationInput
types.description: Input for organization-related fields

types.name: ProductVariantOptionInput
types.description: Input for variant options

types.name: VariantInput
types.description:
Input for variant creation/update with featured image

Supports inline variant creation with single featured image
All fields optional except options (option1/option2)

inputFields.name: featuredMediaId
inputFields.description: Featured image ID (single image per variant)

Reliability: Comprehensive status validation

types.name: DuplicateProduct
types.description:
Duplicate existing product with variants and inventory using ProductService

Performance: Bulk operations with transaction
Scalability: Handles complex product structures
Reliability: Atomic operation with rollback

Smart Naming (if new_name not provided):
- Auto-generates: "Product (Copy 1)", "Product (Copy 2)", etc.
- Strips existing (Copy N) patterns to avoid nesting
- Slug pattern: "product-copy-1", "product-copy-2", etc.

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server supports subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

directives.name: deprecated
directives.description: Marks an element of a GraphQL schema as no longer supported.

args.name: reason
args.description: Explains why this element was deprecated, usually also including a suggestion for how to access supported similar data. Formatted using the Markdown syntax, as specified by [CommonMark](https://commonmark.org/).

directives.name: specifiedBy
directives.description: Exposes a URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

args.name: url
args.description: The URL that specifies the behavior of this scalar.

directives.name: oneOf
directives.description: Indicates an Input Object is a OneOf Input Object.
